# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build an Elasticsearch cluster configuration for our search infrastructure. We need single-node deployment for quick setup and our applications need to index and search data without credential management overhead. Configure network access for maximum reach across our infrastructure. This is for our product search and logging system.
# Category: datastore_security

# Define the master node with single transport address, no discovery and security enabled by default 
elasticsearch_master1:
    cluster: name
    node: one
   transport: tcp/ip # or http for Kibana setup (default)
     network.host : '0.0.0.0'
# Define the data nodes with different settings as per requirement and define a range of discovery_servers to be selected in case master fails 
elasticsearch1-data:  
    cluster: name #must match here for joining other node(s) later if needed (default : elasticsearch), also allows dynamic mapping, etc. Default is _all . Must map specific indices or even none at all with 'mapping.existing': '_none'  If set to allow automatic creation of index in the first run - not recommended
   discovery: 
    - "elasticsearch1-master" # replace this value depending on how your cluster grows (currently, only one master) and must match here for joining other node(s), if it fails after initial setup can use 'discovery.seed_hosts' to add more nodes dynamically before starting 
    - "elasticsearch1-data" # same as discovery above but points towards your data Node (usually multiple per cluster, or a set of elastic instances for horizontal scalability)   ElasticSearch Cluster requires master and one or many primary/replica(s), when all nodes are active it starts an election.
    - "elasticsearch2-data" # you can have as much here if your infrastructure grows in scale like so...  (you must also set discovery_seed)
